text: AI video surveillance (2023-2026): deep learning models (YOLOv8/ReID for multi-object tracking, I3D/VideoMAE for action recognition) analyze CCTV feeds in real-time. Applications: anomaly detection (fighting, loitering, abandoned objects), people counting (retail footfall), vehicle analytics (license plate recognition, parking occupancy), and forensic search ("find person in red shirt between 2-4 PM across 100 cameras").

AI surveillance ethics and regulation: the EU AI Act (2026 enforcement) bans real-time biometric surveillance in public spaces (with exceptions for serious crime). China extensively deploys AI surveillance (Skynet, 600M+ cameras). US cities (San Francisco, Boston) have banned government use of facial recognition. The AI surveillance debate centers on: public safety benefit vs. privacy and civil liberties, bias in facial recognition, and the chilling effect of mass surveillance.
